Summary: In Opalin conditional knockout (cKO) mice, Olig2+ cell numbers in the corpus callosum were decreased compared with control mice. Among Olig2+ cells in the corpus callosum of Opalin cKO mice, the percentage of CC1+ mature oligodendrocytes was reduced. In Opalin icKO mice, the ratio of CC1+/Olig2+ cells to Olig2+ cells in the corpus callosum was significantly decreased.
Reference: Teng, X. Y., Hu, P., Zhang, C. M., Zhang, Q. X., Yang, G., Zang, Y. Y., Liu, Z. X., Chen, G., & Shi, Y. S. (2024). OPALIN is an LGI1 receptor promoting oligodendrocyte differentiation. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A, 121(32), e2403652121. https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2403652121

Summary:
Systematic elaboration.

In Olig2-overexpressing human iPSC cultures with transient, partial Shh inhibition by GANT61 during neural induction, Olig2 drove oligodendroglial specification. qPCR showed that forced Olig2 upregulated NKX2.2, CSPG4, and ST8SIA1 at day 4. Flow cytometry showed Olig2-induced cultures comprised 38.43% NG2+ cells at day 7, compared with 19.94% in control cultures, while PDGFRa+ cells remained largely absent. The qPCR and flow cytometry findings suggested the NG2+ cells were early oligodendroglial lineage cells rather than mature OPCs. In vivo, transplanted Olig2-overexpressing OPCs partly differentiated into MBP+ oligodendrocytes by 8 weeks and engrafted better than control-OPCs. Enhanced engraftment of Olig2-overexpressing OPCs was associated with increased numbers of myelinated axons and thicker myelin sheaths as shown by TEM and lower g-ratio values. In rats with ischemic stroke (tMCAO), transplantation of Olig2-OPCs promoted myelination that resulted in recovery of learning and memory defects in the Morris water maze at 8 weeks and produced greater improvement than control-OPCs and PBS. In OPC-transplanted mice, survival was prolonged over 8 weeks relative to PBS but no significant survival difference was detected between control-OPC and Olig2-OPC groups.
Reference: Xu, J., Zhao, J., Wang, R., Zhang, Y., Shen, L., Xiao, Q., Xie, Y., Jiang, J., Nie, Y., & Deng, W. (2022). Shh and Olig2 sequentially regulate oligodendrocyte differentiation from hiPSCs for the treatment of ischemic stroke. Theranostics, 12(7), 3131-3149. https://doi.org/10.7150/thno.69217

Summary: OLIG2 is a bHLH transcription factor and a master regulator required for oligodendrocyte development. In vivo in mouse NSCs, OPCs, and newly formed oligodendrocytes OLIG2 binds protein-coding and lncRNA genes. OLIG2 binding regions contain motifs for other transcription factors, and OLIG2 can both activate and repress lncRNAs. OLIG2 is involved in post-transcriptional regulation of Olig2 via the 3′ UTR, which contains AU-rich elements, in addition to acting as a genomic transcription factor. ChIP-Seq of H3K27m3 and H3K4m3 marks identified bivalent genes at the NSC, OPC, and NFO stages. Some of those bivalent genes are bound by OLIG2 and are dynamically regulated during oligodendrocyte differentiation. Across NSCs, OPCs, and NFOs OLIG2 called peaks at 613 expressed lncRNAs. OLIG2 appears to repress 39 lncRNAs in NSCs and to activate nine lncRNAs in OPCs and NFOs. In NSCs OLIG2 binds and represses lnc-OPC, and in OPCs OLIG2 does not bind the lnc-OPC upstream regulatory region so lnc-OPC expression increases. Knockdown of lnc-OPC greatly reduced the number of OPCs formed from NSCs. In mice, knockout of Olig2 prevents generation of the oligodendroglial lineage. Specific deletion of Olig2 in OPCs reduced oligodendrocyte differentiation and resulted in hypomyelination. Ablation of Olig2 in immature oligodendrocytes induced oligodendrocyte maturation and promoted myelination and remyelination in mice. Overexpression of Olig2 in neural stem cells promoted generation of OPCs and mature oligodendrocytes in vitro and in vivo.
Reference: Wei, H., Dong, X., You, Y., Hai, B., Duran, R. C., Wu, X., Kharas, N., & Wu, J. Q. (2021). OLIG2 regulates lncRNAs and its own expression during oligodendrocyte lineage formation. BMC Biol, 19(1), 132. https://doi.org/10.1186/s12915-021-01057-6

Summary: During embryonic development, Olig2 is expressed in neural progenitors and specifies neural progenitors into motor neurons and oligodendrocyte precursor cells (OPCs). Olig2 primes oligodendrocyte precursor cells (OPCs) for differentiation into myelin-forming oligodendrocytes. Olig2 is a basic helix–loop–helix transcription factor that dimerizes with itself or other transcription factors and activates or represses target genes in a context-specific manner. Deletion of Olig2 in differentiating oligodendrocytes accelerates their differentiation and myelination and thus illustrates stage-specific opposing effects on oligodendrocyte maturation. Studies examined Olig2‑E1 in proliferating Oli‑neu cells and OPCs and found Olig2‑E1 active in OPCs and oligodendrocytes. OL Hi‑C data show a strong physical interaction between Olig2‑E1 and OLIG2 at 92 Kb. Hi-C data suggest that the physical interaction between Olig2‑E1 and OLIG2 is specific to OL lineage cells. CRISPRi silencing of Olig2‑E1 significantly downregulated Olig2 expression. Luciferase, ATAC‑seq, and ChIP‑seq data support Olig2‑E1 OL specificity and conservation across human, mouse, and rat.
Reference: Fan, C., Kim, D., An, H., & Park, Y. (2023). Identifying an oligodendrocyte enhancer that regulates Olig2 expression. Hum Mol Genet, 32(5), 835-846. https://doi.org/10.1093/hmg/ddac249

Summary: Olig2 is a key transcription factor during oligodendrocyte differentiation. In zebrafish visual morphogenesis olig2-expressing cells (olig2:EGFP) are specified in the preoptic area (POA) and at 2 dpf these OPCs coexpress Sox2 and exhibit round morphology with few processes. At 3 dpf olig2:EGFP/Sox2 cells start acquiring a more elongated morphology. By 5 dpf many olig2:EGFP cells colocalize with sox10:tagRFP and present more projections. At 5 dpf olig2:EGFP cells ensheath the optic nerve and express a myelin marker (mbpa). From 7 dpf most olig2:EGFP cells in the optic nerve colocalize with sox10:tagRFP and lose Sox2 to become fully differentiated oligodendrocytes. From 7 dpf three olig2:EGFP populations coexist in the optic nerve: olig2:EGFP/Sox2, olig2:EGFP/Sox2/sox10:tagRFP, and olig2:EGFP/sox10:tagRFP. Mesencephalic olig2/sox10 oligodendrocytes differentiate earlier than those in the optic nerve.
Reference: Santos-Ledo, A., Pérez-Montes, C., DeOliveira-Mello, L., Arévalo, R., & Velasco, A. (2023). Oligodendrocyte origin and development in the zebrafish visual system. J Comp Neurol, 531(4), 515-527. https://doi.org/10.1002/cne.25440
